items.title: Aston Villa v Everton: Premier League – live
items.summary: ⚽ Premier League updates from the 4.30pm GMT kick-off ⚽ Live scores | Tables | Follow us on Bluesky | Mail Billy Premier League report: Will Unwin was at Molineux this afternoon. Let’s hope this one is a bit livelier. The players are in the tunnel, kick-off is in five minutes. Harrison Armstrong, 18, will start his third game in a row for Everton since returning from his loan at Preston. Continue reading...

items.title: VI Live: Malen scoort direct bij AS Roma-debuut, Saibari in de basis
items.summary: Zondag is het Derby Day in de Eredivisie. We trappen af met de Derby van het Noorden tussen SC Heerenveen en FC Groningen, vervolgen met de Twentse Derby Heracles Almelo - FC Twente en sluiten af met het treffen tussen Feyenoord en Sparta Rotterdam, dat in een apart liveblog wordt behandeld. Verder is er ook buitenlands voetbal. Volg het hier in de VI Live.

items.title: Aston Villa verzuimt dichter bij titeldroom te komen na puntenverlies concurrenten
items.summary: Aston Villa heeft verzuimd om te profiteren van het puntenverlies dat titelkandidaten Arsenal en Manchester City leden dit Premier League-weekend. Op zondag ging de ploeg van trainer Unai Emery pijnlijk onderuit tegen het effectieve Everton.

items.title: 'Als je Van Persie wegstuurt, heb je geen idee wat dat oplevert'
items.summary: Robin van Persie is volgens Mario Been niet als enige verantwoordelijk voor de slechte resultaten van Feyenoord. De oud-trainer van de Rotterdammers stelt zijn vraagtekens bij de totstandkoming van de selectie.

items.title: Wellenreuther baalt van open huis bij Feyenoord: 'Er zijn twee doelen op het veld'
items.summary: De teleurstelling was van het gezicht van Timon Wellenreuther af te scheppen zondagavond. Feyenoord verloor in de laatste seconden de Rotterdamse derby van Sparta . Maar dat dat eigenlijk verdiend was ook, stak het meest bij de Duitse doelman.

items.title: AFCON final: Senegal vs Morocco team news LIVE!
Utd's Martinez hits back over 'toddler' criticism
items.summary: Lisandro Martínez has challenged Paul Scholes and Nicky Butt to repeat their criticism to his face after the pair said Erling Haaland would make the Manchester United defender look like "a little toddler" in the Manchester derby.

items.title: 'Sensational!' | Barry squeezes it in from tight angle to give Everton lead!
items.summary: Thierno Barry gives Everton the lead at Villa Park.

items.title: Ajax hoping to secure loan move for Zinchenko
items.summary: Ajax are in talks over a loan move for Arsenal defender Oleksandr Zinchenko.

items.title: Robin van Persie krijgt Feyenoord niet op één lijn
items.summary: Zelfs twee goals van gouden wissel Shaqueel van Persie hebben een nederlaag van Feyenoord tegen Sparta Rotterdam niet kunnen voorkomen: 3-4. Tijd voor een analyse van de verdedigende problemen van het elftal van Robin van Persie.

items.title: Government pulls amendment to Hillsborough law after backlash
items.summary: Proposed change relating to spies was criticised by campaigners and MPs as allowing an opt-out for senior officials The government has pulled an amendment to its proposed Hillsborough law amid concerns from campaigners and MPs that the legislation was being watered down. The public office (accountability) bill aims to force public officials and contractors to tell the truth after disasters. Continue reading...

items.title: Goals galore in FA Cup as Liverpool, Man Utd & Man City progress
items.summary: Liverpool, Manchester City and Manchester United all cruise into the fifth round of the Women's FA Cup as 55 goals are scored across 12 matches on Sunday.

items.title: 'Moyes will be furious!' | Was Everton goal wrongly disallowed?
items.summary: Jake O'Brien's goal for Everton against Aston Villa is disallowed.

items.title: Everton hit the post seconds after kick-off!
items.summary: Everton almost take an early lead at Villa Park!

items.title: Newcastle struggle to find cutting edge in drab stalemate with Wolves
items.summary: Newcastle arrived at Molineux in retro shirts to bring back memories of David Ginola and Les Ferdinand but there was neither swagger nor flair in this goalless draw against Wolves. There was the potential to go fifth in the Premier League with a win for the Magpies but instead they toiled on their way to a point. With two-thirds of possession throughout, Newcastle must have expected to come away with more but they possessed neither the creativity nor finishing. Wolves were worthy of the draw, which moves them three points away from Derby’s worst tally of 11, a total many home supporters feared would not be overcome at Christmas. Continue reading...

items.title: Protektorat passes £1m mark with Fleur de Lys win
items.summary: Harry Skelton hailed Protektorat a “warrior” after he successfully defended his crown in the Fitzdares Fleur de Lys Chase at Windsor.

items.title: Police investigating elbow incident in grassroots match
items.summary: Police are investigating an incident in which a football player appeared to deliberately elbow an opponent in the face during a Welsh grassroots match.

items.title: Sources: PSG finalizing deal for Barcelona starlet
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain are finalising the remaining details for the transfer of Dro Fernández, the latest prodigy from Barcelona's academy, sources confirmed to ESPN.

items.title: Arteta: Arsenal denied clear penalty in Forest draw
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has said Arsenal were denied a "very clear penalty" in Saturday's 0-0 draw at Nottingham Forest.
